steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage presents a clear cause-and-effect structure: the 2023 measurements revealed a concerning rotation (cause), and the blank introduces the city officials’ response — closing the area around the tower (effect). The correct transition must signal that the closure was a direct consequence of the alarming measurements.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In comparison,’: signals that two things are being compared side by side. No second comparable situation is introduced — the closure is a response to the rotation, not a parallel case. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘As a result,’: signals that what follows is a direct consequence of what came before. The closure is the direct consequence of the concerning rotation — exactly the cause-effect relationship in the passage. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘For example,’: signals that what follows is a specific instance of a broader pattern. No broader pattern has been stated; the closure is not an example of anything — it is the result of the rotation. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Similarly,’: signals that two situations are parallel. The closure is not parallel to the rotation; it is the response to it. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
